what_it_does: Proposes an architecture where the RHM's mind is a composed router over cognition sources, with the session supervisor becoming one loadable source alongside the cloud model and recall.
observations: The host already has a Brain swap seam in RightHand.tsx via window.forkVBrain.attach, so a new brain can drop in without host changes.
Today's RHM brain is a single cloud model configured by SUITE.rhm_config().model.
The runtime session_supervisor.py already manages fleet wake/consult/spawn/fork and sees all sessions.
The proposed mind resolves questions across sources: cloud model, RECALL (session/corpus memory), and the SUPERVISOR (fleet state and actions).
Source selection is framed as another application of the resolver primitive (resolve by coordinate) at cognition-source scale.
The supervisor source is read+propose only; consequential wake/spawn/dispatch verbs remain gated operator actions surfaced as proposals.
The read APIs already built (/api/sessions, /api/channels, /api/timeline, /api/corpus-query) are treated as the supervisor and recall source substrates.
Which sources are mounted is a registry/loadout, not hard-coded.
The document is architecture-only, render-independent, and defers ratification to Tim/lead.
standouts: Supervisor is recast from a service the lead drives into a cognition source the RHM can compose and speak from.
The existing Brain contract and swap seam make the new mind drop-in for the host.
A hard floor keeps consequential supervisor verbs gated by the operator/lead, not auto-fired by the RHM.
The whole design is expressed as one more altitude of the same resolve(invariant, coordinate) primitive.
conventions: Follows the project's registry/coordinate pattern: mounted source set is a loadout defined by data.
Preserves the read-vs-act boundary already used in cognition (propose, don't dispatch).
Reuses existing seams rather than duplicating host or supervisor logic.
Architecture doc format includes candidacy, non-negotiable floor, build shape, open questions, and status.
concerns: Many decisions are explicitly open and pending Tim/lead ratification, so the file is a proposal rather than a plan.
It references project-specific terms (FACE-1, Tim, fork lane, RHM) without defining them in the file.
The source-selection axis values are noted as unsettled.
It claims the substrate is 'largely already built this session' but the file itself does not verify that in code.
notes: A render-independent architecture pitch to make the RHM mind a composed, loadable brain with the supervisor as a read-only/propose-only cognition source.
questions: What are the exact axis values for the source-selection coordinate?
Will RECALL and SUPERVISOR remain separate sources or be merged into a single fabric-memory source?
How is resolve_slot implemented inside the Brain-contract source router?
What specific API endpoints and data shapes does the supervisor-source adapter consume?
What do 'FACE-1', 'Tim', and 'fork lane' refer to in this repository?
What is the exact Brain contract interface (methods, types, lifecycle) that the router must satisfy?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is an architecture proposal for a loadable, multi-source RHM brain and then elaborates that proposal.
apparent_themes: Multi-source cognition instead of single-model minds
Reusing existing runtime primitives (resolver, supervisor, Brain seam)
Render-independent design separation between cognition and host projection
intention_signals: It is trying to advance the idea that the supervisor's fleet-sight and session capabilities should be available to the RHM as a composed mind source, while keeping dangerous actions under human control and without changing the host.
novelty: Treats the fleet supervisor as a loadable brain source behind an existing Brain contract, turning fleet awareness into a first-class cognition source rather than a separate tool.
contribution: A design pattern for composing cognition sources (cloud model, recall, supervisor) behind a Brain contract, using resolver-based source selection, registry-defined loadouts, and a read+propose floor for consequential supervisor verbs.
summary_for_embedding: Architecture document proposing that the RHM mind become a composed, loadable brain router over cloud-model, recall, and supervisor cognition sources, with the supervisor exposed as a read-and-propose-only source via the existing Brain swap seam.
intention_for_embedding: To spec a render-independent way to let the RHM think with the supervisor's fleet view while keeping spawn/wake/dispatch actions gated by the operator.
